name: Twin Bridges Canoe & Campground
description: Riverside resort at the SH 14 bridge (river mile 24.1) east of Dora; canoe & kayak floats, cabins, full-hookup tent/RV camping, showers, camp store, and the adjacent Twin Bridges Bar & Grill (open Tue-Sun). Popular trips: Hebron to Twin Bridges (5.4 mi) and Twin Bridges to Hammond/NFRA (5 mi).

name: River of Life Farm
description: 500-acre trout resort at river mile 35 famous for stilted log-cabin treehouses over the North Fork ('miracle mile') and fly-fishing guides. Public float rentals (canoe/kayak/raft/tube) and shuttles. Trips: Twin Bridges to ROLF (12 mi) and Hammond to ROLF (7 mi). The Falls ledge is in front of the resort.

name: Pettit's Canoe & Campground
description: Family-run canoe rental & campground at Blair Bridge (river mile ~39.9, river right). Canoe/kayak/5-person raft/tube rentals; tent & RV camping with flush toilets, hot showers, hookups, fire pits and free firewood; four cabins (sleep 6-8). Trips: Hammond to Pettit's and Pettit's to Dawt Mill (7 mi).

name: Sunburst Ranch
description: Resort with a mile of river frontage just north of Patrick Bridge (river mile ~43.6); cabins, lodges and a renovated airplane-hangar (sleeps 8-16), plus primitive/electric/full-hookup RV camping. Canoe/kayak/raft/tube rentals include shuttle. No vehicle shuttle (insurance). Second access 'The Landing' 7 mi downstream. Fly-fishing guides.

name: Dawt Mill Resort
description: Historic grist-mill resort (southernmost on the river, river mile ~47) with lodging (Cotton Gin Inn plus new luxury suites), the seasonal Beach Bar & food truck, the Chef's Table fine-dining, and a general store. Canoe/kayak/raft/tube rentals for the Blair Bridge to Dawt float (8 mi), launched hourly 9am-noon. Private boats: registered guests only (post-2017 insurance).
seasonal_notes: Beach Bar Memorial Day to Labor Day

name: North Fork Recreation Area (Hammond Camp)
description: USFS river access & campground in Mark Twain National Forest at river mile 29.2 (locally 'Hammond'); heavily used put-in/take-out. 20 first-come sites (one 30-amp electric), vault toilets, drinking water, boat launch and a swim area; the 0.5-mi Blue Spring Trail. $2/vehicle day-use fee (annual pass $20). Gate opens Mar 1 (day-use) / May 15 (camping).
seasonal_notes: Campground open May 15 to Nov 30; day-use from Mar 1

name: Patrick Bridge Campground
description: MDC public access & campground at river mile 42.1 on Highway H near Caulfield (161 acres); a popular summer swimming/water-play spot. 13 first-come campsites with concrete tables, fire rings and trash poles; two pit toilets; gravel boat ramp. Trail to Althea Spring (Missouri's 23rd largest) and an old mill-dam waterfall. Little shade in midsummer.
